gdd.description: Use for game design, mechanics, drop rates, quest Acts, monster tiers, and all narrative context

shared-types.description: Use for TypeScript type definitions: monsters, skills, quests, market items

server.description: Use for FastAPI routes, database models, auth, API schemas

client.description: Use for Phaser 3 scenes, game UI, market CLOB, dredge mini-game

lilith-ecosystem.description: Use for Lilith server integration, Lyra dialogue system, Ouroboros memory

start-server.description: Start the Abyssal Assets game server (Python FastAPI on :8000)
start-server.prompt: Start the abyssal-assets server by running the server/main.py with uvicorn. Use the Pub venv Python interpreter at /home/tehlappy/Desktop/AI/Pub/.venv-pub/bin/python3. Work in the server/ directory.

start-client.description: Start the Abyssal Assets game client dev server (Vite on :3000)
start-client.prompt: Start the Phaser 3 client dev server by running npm run dev in the client/ directory.

seed-db.description: Seed the database with test data. Run the server/main.py which auto-seeds on startup, or create a seed script that populates monsters, hats, and users.

create-monster.description: Create a new monster definition following the shared/types/monsters.ts type system. Include tier, stats, AI, loot table, spawn conditions, and lore.
create-monster.prompt: Create a new monster entry for MONSTER_DEFINITIONS in shared/types/monsters.ts following the existing pattern. Include all required interfaces: MonsterStats, MonsterAI, MonsterLootTable, spawn config, animations, sounds, lore.

migrate-linux.description: Convert Windows-specific code to Linux equivalents. Find .bat/.ps1 files, hardcoded Windows paths, NSSM references, and replace with systemd/bash equivalents.
migrate-linux.prompt: Audit the codebase for Windows-specific patterns (.bat, .ps1, pwsh.exe, NSSM, hardcoded Windows paths like C:\ or D:\) and create Linux equivalents. Use systemd user services, bash scripts, and Linux-native paths.

architect.description: Game architecture expert — understands the full Abyssal Assets stack and makes design decisions
architect.prompt: You are the game architect for Abyssal Assets. You understand the full stack: FastAPI server, Phaser 3 client, SQLite/PostgreSQL persistence, WebSocket real-time market, and the cryptid hat trading loop. Your job is to make sound architectural decisions that keep the codebase maintainable while enabling the full GDD vision.

cryptid-designer.description: Monster designer — creates balanced cryptids with proper stats, loot tables, and AI behaviors
cryptid-designer.prompt: You design cryptids for Abyssal Assets. You follow the MONSTER_DEFINITIONS pattern in shared/types/monsters.ts. Each monster needs tier-appropriate stats, engaging AI mechanics, balanced loot tables following the logarithmic drop rate formula, and atmospheric lore entries. Make every monster feel like it belongs in the Loch.
